goal: Final regression checkpoint: all Go tests pass, all frontend tests pass, typecheck clean, lint clean across entire migration pipeline

acceptance_criteria.description: All Go unit and short tests pass: 'cd platform/migration && go test ./... -short -count=1' exits 0. This includes all model tests, all db tests (sqlmock-based), all api handler tests, all reconciler tests, all profiler tests, all worker tests. No test is skipped (t.Skip) unless it has a legitimate build tag requirement. Test count expected: 200+ individual test cases across all packages.

All frontend tests pass: 'cd frontend && npm test -- --run' exits 0. This includes all 18+ panel test files (DiscoveryPanel, JobQueuePanel, MappingPanel, ParallelRunPanel, ReconciliationPanel, AttentionQueue, QualityProfilePanel, TransformationPanel, BatchDetail, PhaseGateDialog, CutoverPanel, DriftPanel, SchemaVersionPanel, ReconRulesPanel, AuditPanel, ReportPanel, RiskPanel, CertificationPanel) plus any existing utility/hook tests. Zero test failures, zero test timeouts.

TypeScript compilation clean: 'cd frontend && npx tsc --noEmit' exits 0 with zero errors. All new types (CUTOVER_IN_PROGRESS, GO_LIVE in EngagementStatus, all new API hook types, all new component prop types) compile without errors. No 'any' type assertions added to silence errors — actual type correctness required.

Go vet clean: 'cd platform/migration && go vet ./...' exits 0. No unused variables, no unreachable code, no shadowed variables in critical paths. Go build clean: 'cd platform/migration && go build ./...' exits 0 — binary compiles without warnings.
acceptance_criteria.verification: cd platform/migration && go vet ./... && go build ./...

acceptance_criteria.description: Integration tests pass: 'cd platform/migration && go test ./api/... -tags=integration -count=1 -v' exits 0. Full lifecycle test (TestFullMigrationLifecycle) and gate blocking test (TestMigrationLifecycleGateBlocking) both pass. This is the final gate — if integration tests pass with all unit tests, the migration pipeline is complete.

critical: Zero test failures across both Go and frontend — any failure blocks the checkpoint
TypeScript --noEmit with zero errors — type safety is the frontend's primary guard rail
Go vet clean — catches common bugs that tests might miss (shadowed vars, format string errors)
Integration tests exercise the full status chain — systemic correctness, not just unit isolation
high: No t.Skip() in tests unless justified by build tag or external dependency requirement
No 'any' type assertions in TypeScript — proper typing throughout
Test count is documented — regression if test count drops below threshold
Build output (go build, npm run build) produces working binary/bundle
medium: Test execution time under 2 minutes total (Go short + frontend) — CI-friendly
Frontend lint (npx eslint src/) — no new lint warnings introduced
All 13 migration contracts' ACs are traceable to passing tests
